openQuestions.question: Q1: where is the Send-issue commit in the new Engine? Prototype stages but has no send control (C15).
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Reuse HEAD's docked send bar verbatim over POST /bulk (doc's own quick-win row rates this zero-risk).

openQuestions.question: Q2: does the Engine absorb the full curation machinery (C2–C11) or does curation live elsewhere?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Move-row Drawer on the Engine exposing all verbs (T1 carry-over-then-reskin); hard floor: no verb dropped.

openQuestions.question: Q3: redirect policy for old seo-strategy?tab= URLs.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Redirect map to Keywords/Pipeline/Competitors per D8 (required deliverable anyway).

openQuestions.question: Q4: homes for trust ladder (E3), setup readiness (E4), leads readout (E5), doorbell target (E1), outcome-value enrich (E6).
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Engine collapsed 'Operations' Disclosure section; E6 to wsettings next to outcomeValue.

openQuestions.question: Q5: where do the 4 cannibalization sub-verbs (keeper override / resolve / fix-in-editor / send) land when the card folds into a backing move?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Inside the move-row Drawer for cannibalization-archetype moves.

openQuestions.question: Q6: sign off the money-frame + verdict data contract (which server fields, which basis pill) before any admin dollar figure renders.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: sn-engine-1 fields with provenance pill mirroring the client Measured/Estimate pill.

openQuestions.question: Q7: Diagnostics' fate (ledger Gap) before wiring the per-move deep-diagnostic hook (E8).
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep Diagnostics as an admin surface; wire E8 to existing POST /api/jobs 'deep-diagnostic'.

openQuestions.question: Q8: keep full-vs-incremental generation + RefreshOrderingPrompt, or collapse to one Regenerate + cadence?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep both (collapsing silently changes cost/latency and drops the local-data-ordering guard).

openQuestions.question: Q9: is E2 promotion ClientKeywordFeedback-approve rebranded, or a new inbox-request→rec path (sn-engine-3)?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Rebrand feedback-approve + manual-rec for v1; new generic path only if inbox requests must promote.

openQuestions.question: Q10: confirm Keywords/SEO Editor audits claim pageMap so the Engine can drop page↔keyword mapping without loss.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Yes per D3 (Page Intelligence → SEO Editor Research mode); verify at those audits' outputs.

openQuestions.question: NEW: strategy-signal-fold (D6) — signals stay a panel or fold into recs? Building then folding is double work.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep the panel; retire the fold flag as not-pursued.

openQuestions.question: NEW: money-frame read-safety — computeROI writes a snapshot on call; the Engine header must not snapshot per render.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Cron-precomputed value (return-hook-cron pattern) read by sn-engine-1.

verdicts.evidence: server/roi.ts:411 (saveSnapshot inside computeROI, confirming write-on-read); server/roi.ts:123 + shared/types/roi.ts:37 (revenueAtStake already computed); server/routes/the-issue-admin.ts (46 lines, no money read); server/the-issue-return-hook.ts:6-7 + server/return-hook-cron.ts:119-122 (return-hook precompute pattern is real)
verdicts.correction: Direction and read-safety concern are CORRECT — computeROI writes a snapshot (server/roi.ts:411) so it must not run on a hot GET, and the return-hook cron is the proven precompute pattern. But the claim understates what exists: the 'pipeline-value-at-stake' number ALREADY exists as ROIData.revenueAtStake (server/roi.ts:123, shared/types/roi.ts:37, rendered in src/components/client/ROIDashboard.tsx:265). Only 'recovered-so-far' and the 'basis pill' are genuinely absent (rg confirms zero hits repo-wide). So the header needs a read-safe money PROJECTION that surfaces the already-computed revenueAtStake plus two new derived fields — not a from-scratch value-at-stake computation. Effort M still fair given the cron/snapshot precompute plumbing, but the value-at-stake half is a reuse, not a build.

verdicts.evidence: shared/types/strategy-pov.ts:13-29 (StrategyPov: situation/leadMoveRecId/leadSentence/wins/flags/version — no verdict/headline); shared/types/strategy-pov.ts:31-37 (StrategyPovAIOutput likewise lacks it); server/strategy-pov-store.ts:18,36-41,106 (stored as pov_json JSON blob, not columns — additive field needs no migration); routes exist at server/routes/strategy-pov.ts
verdicts.correction: Stands as written. POV generate/store/route pipeline exists; no verdict/headline field. Because POV persists as a pov_json blob (server/strategy-pov-store.ts:106), the additive field needs NO migration/mapper lockstep — effort S is correct. Multi-touch caveat for the plan: the field must be added to StrategyPov, StrategyPovAIOutput, the strategyPovSchema Zod schema + fallback object (server/strategy-pov-store.ts:51-53,60), and the generator prompt — but all in one file-cluster, still S.

verdicts.evidence: shared/types/recommendations.ts:29 (source is a free-form `string`, NOT a union); server/domains/recommendations/route-mutations.ts:122 mintManualRecommendation mints source:'manual:<hex>'; server/routes/recommendations.ts:806 POST /manual-rec; shared/types/client-actions.ts:8-13 ClientActionSourceType is a 5-member union with none for client-request-promotion; server/routes/keyword-strategy.ts:623 keyword-feedback approves keywords into rank tracking, not a rec mint
verdicts.correction: Right direction, one wrong detail. mintManualRecommendation (server/domains/recommendations/route-mutations.ts:122) IS the correct reusable base — the 'reduce to reusing manual-rec' hedge is well-founded. BUT the whereItLives claim of a 'shared/types/recommendations.ts source union' to widen is WRONG: Recommendation.source is a free-form string (shared/types/recommendations.ts:29), so there is no union to extend — a promotion just passes a distinct source string (e.g. 'client_request:<id>'). No existing client-request→rec provenance link exists anywhere. Effort M holds because honest attribution needs the ephemeral-source snapshot-ref pattern (CLAUDE.md rule) to record which inbox request minted the rec, not because of any union migration.

verdicts.evidence: server/the-issue-export.ts:57-60 assembleOnePagerExport calls computeROI (which WRITES — server/roi.ts:411); server/routes/the-issue-export.ts:39,58 BOTH routes use requireAuthenticatedClientPortalAuth() (confirmed at those lines), NOT admin auth; src/components/client/the-issue/TheIssueClientPage.tsx already has a previewMode prop that mounts read-only; src/components/strategy/issue/IssueHeader.tsx:20-21 notes the preview-as-client toggle was REMOVED as a dead control awaiting a client surface; client spine reads via public endpoint /api/public/recommendations/:workspaceId?clientStatus=sent (src/api/theIssue.ts:50)
verdicts.correction: Direction right (an admin spine preview does not exist and the export assembler is the reuse candidate), but two evidence errors and a missed hazard. (1) The cited lines server/routes/the-issue-export.ts:37,56 are NOT 'admin export routes' — both are requireAuthenticatedClientPortalAuth() (client-portal auth), so they cannot serve an admin render as-is. (2) The reusable assembler assembleOnePagerExport calls computeROI (server/the-issue-export.ts:60), which writes a snapshot (server/roi.ts:411) — so reusing it for a hot admin preview GET hits the SAME read-safety landmine as sn-engine-1; the claim calls it 'admin-reusable' without flagging this. Partial groundwork already exists frontend-side: TheIssueClientPage supports previewMode, and IssueHeader documents the removed toggle — the real gap is an admin-AUTH data source (read-safe, not the write-path assembler) to feed that preview. Effort S optimistic if the read must be made write-free; realistically S→M.
